data.title: As a user, I get a single batched summary confirmation instead of per-file 'Does this look right?' prompts
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace the per-file "Does this look right? Should I write this file?" confirmation pattern with a batched summary confirmation in standard mode
**Why**: All users experience unnecessary friction from confirming every single file write individually
**Scope**:
- IN: Summary listing all files to write with one-line descriptions, one confirmation prompt, all files written after confirmation. Phase 2 per-recommendation accept/reject is preserved unchanged
- OUT: --auto mode (separate story), re-run merge logic (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, architect-foundation presents a summary of all files it intends to write with a brief description of each, gets one confirmation, then writes them all
  ← Satisfies: "file writes are batched: the skill presents a summary listing all files"
- When this story is complete, Phase 2 per-recommendation accept/reject interaction is unchanged
  ← Satisfies: "Phase 2 per-recommendation accept/reject is preserved unchanged"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Behavioral testing to verify single confirmation pattern replaces per-file prompts

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
